title: STORYTELLING AS A METHOD: THE ROLE OF NARRATIVE IN ENHANCING LISTENING PROFICIENCY IN SECONDARY SCHOOL EFL LEARNERS
author: Talapova A.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This article examines the effectiveness of storytelling as a pedagogical method in enhancing listening proficiency among secondary school EFL learners. Grounded in communicative and constructivist learning theories, this research explores the role of storytelling as a pedagogical method in enhancing listening proficiency and learner engagement among secondary school EFL students. The study employed a quantitative approach, using an online survey distributed to 50 participants, including both English teachers and students from Almaty secondary schools. The questionnaire combined Likert-scale, multiple-choice, and open-ended questions to investigate participants’ perceptions of storytelling’s effectiveness in developing listening comprehension, motivation, and classroom participation. The results indicate that storytelling contributes to a deeper understanding of spoken language, increases students’ motivation, and fosters interactive classroom communication. However, the findings also reveal certain challenges, such as limited teaching time, a lack of authentic materials, and insufficient teacher preparation. Overall, the study highlights storytelling’s potential as an engaging and effective instructional strategy in EFL listening lessons and emphasizes the importance of teacher training and resource development for its successful implementation.

keywords: Storytelling, listening proficiency, EFL learners, secondary education, motivation, narrative pedagogy, classroom engagement.
